title: Ensure that the admin.conf file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the admin.conf file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the admin.conf file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The admin.conf is the administrator kubeconfig file defining various settings for the administration of the cluster. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: In OpenShift 4 the admin config file is stored in /etc/kubernetes/kubeconfig with permissions 644 . Run the following command. for i in $(oc get nodes -o name) do echo $i oc debug $i -- <<EOF chroot /host stat -c%a /etc/kubernetes/kubeconfig EOF done Verify that the permissions are 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: No remediation required; file permissions are managed by the operator.
input_row.default_value: By default, in OpenShift 4, kubeconfig has permissions of 644 . 43 | P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the OpenShift Cluster:**
   - Use the OpenShift CLI (oc) to log in to your OpenShift cluster using an administration account.

2. **Retrieve Node Names:**
   - Execute the command: `oc get nodes -o name`
   - This command will list all nodes in the OpenShift cluster.

3. **Check File Permissions:**
   - For each node, run the command:
     ```bash
     for i in $(oc get nodes -o name); do
       oc debug $i -- <<EOF
       chroot /host stat -c%a /etc/kubernetes/kubeconfig
       EOF
     done
     ```
   - This command will check the file permissions of the `admin.conf` located at `/etc/kubernetes/kubeconfig` on each node in the cluster.

4. **Verify Permissions:**
   - After executing the above commands, check the output for each node to see the permissions.
   - Ensure that the permissions display `644` or more restrictive values (e.g., `600`, `640`).

5. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - Document the permissions for each node.
   - If any node displays permissions less restrictive than `644`, it indicates non-compliance with the control.

6. **Review Documentation:**
   - As a reference, you can review the OpenShift documentation you might have accessed to ensure that you’re interpreting the results correctly.
   - Useful links include:
     - [OpenShift CLI Reference](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.5/cli_reference/openshift_cli/administrator-cli-commands.html)
     - [Kubernetes Setup](https://kubernetes.io/docs/setup/production-environment/tools/kubeadm/create-cluster-kubeadm/)

**Expected Secure Configuration:**
- The expected output for secure configurations should show that file permissions are `644`, `640`, or `600` for the `admin.conf` file across all nodes.

**How to Confirm Compliance:**
- If all nodes report the correct permissions, document this as compliant. If any nodes do not meet the requirement, escalate for remediation based on organization policy.
